user.title: Aeolus
user.description: This creates the Aeolus manager. After you press Submit, open the new Aeolus entry and use **+ Add** to create **Spaces** (zones you want to manage by CO₂) and **Actuators** (fans, vents, ERV or windows that lower CO₂). Nothing is controlled until you add and configure those.

abort.already_configured: Aeolus is already set up. Add Spaces and Actuators from the Aeolus entry's ⋮ menu.

init.title: Aeolus options
init.description: Advanced features. Leave these off for a simple CO₂ setup.

data_description.enable_ladders: Adds a step when you create or edit a Space that lets you build a multi-tier particulate/AQI response (e.g. PM2.5 > 30 → hood 20%; > 80 → everything 100%). Off keeps the Space form to just CO₂. Turning this off later never deletes ladders you've already built — they keep running; you just can't re-author them until you turn it back on.

user.title: Add a space
user.description: A **Space** is a zone managed by CO₂ (e.g. Primary Bedroom). Choose its CO₂ sensor(s) and the levels Aeolus should hold. Optionally add a graduated PM/AQI response too.

data_description.name: A friendly name for this zone — it becomes the device name.
data_description.co2_sensors: One or more CO₂ sensors in this zone. Several are smoothed together into one reading. Leave empty for a particulate-only space.
data_description.aggregation: How to combine multiple sensors: Mean = average; Median = ignores a single outlier; Min/Max = best / worst-case.
data_description.target_ppm: Ventilation stops once the zone reaches this. ~800 ppm is good indoor air; fresh outdoor air is ~420 ppm.
data_description.high_ppm: Ventilation starts when the zone rises above this. Must be higher than the target — the gap between them is the deadband that prevents short-cycling.
data_description.volume_ft3: Optional, and currently NOT used. Reserved for the planned occupancy/generation (equilibrium-CO₂) estimate. The air-change-rate readout is gap-normalized and does not need volume.
data_description.outdoor_aq_entity: Optional. A PM2.5 sensor measuring this zone's outdoor air. When it's high, Aeolus won't pull outdoor air in.
data_description.outdoor_aq_threshold: Optional. Block outdoor-air ventilation when the outdoor PM sensor exceeds this value.
data_description.add_graduated: Turn on to also drive actuators by a particulate/AQI ladder (e.g. PM2.5 > 30 → hood 20% + purifiers 33%; > 80 → everything 100%). The CO₂ settings above still apply.

reconfigure.title: Edit space
reconfigure.description: Update this zone's sensors and CO₂ levels. Turn on the graduated response to re-author its PM/AQI ladder (this replaces the existing one); leave it off to keep the current ladder.

data_description.name: A friendly name for this zone — it becomes the device name.
data_description.co2_sensors: One or more CO₂ sensors in this zone. Several are smoothed together into one reading.
data_description.aggregation: How to combine multiple sensors: Mean = average; Median = ignores a single outlier; Min/Max = best / worst-case.
data_description.target_ppm: Ventilation stops once the zone reaches this. ~800 ppm is good indoor air; fresh outdoor air is ~420 ppm.
data_description.high_ppm: Ventilation starts when the zone rises above this. Must be higher than the target.
data_description.volume_ft3: Optional, currently not used (reserved for the planned generation/equilibrium-CO₂ estimate). The ACH readout is gap-normalized — volume-free.
data_description.outdoor_aq_entity: Optional. A PM2.5 sensor measuring this zone's outdoor air.
data_description.outdoor_aq_threshold: Optional. Block outdoor-air ventilation when the outdoor PM sensor exceeds this value.
data_description.add_graduated: On = walk through the metric + tiers again, replacing the saved ladder. Off = keep the existing ladder unchanged.

metric.title: Graduated response — pollutant
metric.description: Pick the particulate/AQI metric this ladder responds to, and its sensor(s). On the next screen(s) you'll set the response tiers.

data_description.kind: What this ladder watches — PM1, PM2.5, PM10, an AQI, or any numeric sensor.
data_description.sensors: One or more sensors for this pollutant in this zone.
data_description.aggregation: How to combine them. **Maximum** = act if ANY sensor exceeds a tier (recommended for particulates).

tier.title: Graduated response — tier {tier}
tier.description: When the pollutant rises above the engage level, each actuator runs at the speed you set here (0 = off at this tier). Add higher tiers for stronger responses. Levels ramp back down automatically as the pollutant clears.

data_description.engage_at: The pollutant level (µg/m³ or AQI) at which this tier turns on. Release (turn-off) is automatic, ~15% below this.
data_description.add_another_tier: Turn on to define a higher tier after this one; leave off to finish.

user.title: Add an actuator
user.description: An **Actuator** moves or filters air. Fans/vents/ERV/windows lower CO₂ **and** particulates; a recirculating **air purifier** (mechanism *Filter*) lowers particulates only — it does **not** remove CO₂.

data_description.name: A friendly name for this device.
data_description.actuator_entity: The fan, switch, input_boolean, or cover Aeolus controls. (One actuator can drive a group later.)
data_description.mechanism: Balanced = ERV/HRV, neutral pressure. Supply = pushes outdoor air in. Exhaust = pulls air out (depressurizes). Transfer = moves air between rooms. Window = an opener/cover. Filter = recirculating air purifier (PM only, not CO₂).
data_description.served_spaces: Which Space(s) this device ventilates for CO₂ — Aeolus runs it when one of them is above its high threshold. (For particulate ladders, assign the actuator inside the Space's graduated response instead.)
data_description.filter_efficiency: How well this device's intake filters fine particles: 0 = none/foam, ~0.5 = MERV-13, 0.9+ = HEPA. A filtered intake is allowed to run when outdoor air is dirtier.
data_description.rearm_interval: For a fan/switch whose load auto-offs on its own while the switch keeps reporting 'on', re-send the ON command this often (minutes) while still needed. 0 = off.
data_description.on_speed_pct: Fans only — the speed for the simple CO₂ on/off case (0 = the fan's default). Graduated PM tiers set their own per-tier speeds.
data_description.override_grace_min: How long a hand/automation change must persist before Aeolus yields. 0 = immediate. Set ~2 for cloud devices (e.g. LG ThinQ) whose state briefly flaps.

reconfigure.title: Edit actuator
reconfigure.description: Update this device, how it moves air, and the spaces it serves.

data_description.name: A friendly name for this device.
data_description.actuator_entity: The fan, switch, input_boolean, or cover Aeolus turns on/off.
data_description.mechanism: Balanced = ERV/HRV. Supply = pushes outdoor air in. Exhaust = pulls air out. Transfer = between rooms. Window = opener/cover. Filter = recirculating purifier (PM only, not CO₂).
data_description.served_spaces: Which Space(s) this device ventilates for CO₂.
data_description.filter_efficiency: Intake fine-particle filtration: 0 = none/foam, ~0.5 = MERV-13, 0.9+ = HEPA.
data_description.rearm_interval: Re-send the ON command this often (minutes) for a load that auto-offs internally. 0 = off.
data_description.on_speed_pct: Fans only — % speed for the simple CO₂ case (0 = fan default). PM tiers set their own speeds.
data_description.override_grace_min: Minutes a manual change must persist before Aeolus yields. 0 = immediate. ~2 for cloud actuators (LG ThinQ).

recalibrate.name: Recalibrate
recalibrate.description: Clear observed/learned actuator effectiveness for an Aeolus entry.

config_entry_id.name: Config entry
config_entry_id.description: The Aeolus config entry to recalibrate.
